meaning: n.业余艺术爱好者adj.业余艺术爱好的
examples: He is a master of that area even if he is a dilettante.
It is also a phenomenological layers which encourage dilettante contemplation.
I'm too serious to be a dilettante and too much a dabbler to be a professional.
Now this obscure writer's book has been transformed into a brilliant movie by Julian Schnabel, the artist and cinematic dilettante.
' I've worked very hard to get away from the impression that I'm some sort of dilettante riding on my inheritance.
